group_info.group: 3 adults + 3 teens (13-15), able-bodied, good travel problem-solvers

objectives.name: Exploration index
objectives.desc: Places none of us have been. Greece = high (first time) for everyone. Rome, French Riviera, and Italian Riviera are all lower here since 2 of the 6 travelers have already been to all three.

objectives.name: History & culture index
objectives.desc: Real ruins, castles, archaeological sites -- walk through the story, not just photos. Note: teens ranked ancient ruins LOWEST of 5 priorities when asked what they'd miss most if cut -- adults may value this more than teens do.

objectives.name: Outdoor / active index
objectives.desc: Hiking, swimming, physical challenge. Teens want to move.

objectives.name: Mediterranean coast index
objectives.desc: Turquoise water, beaches, cliffs -- a significant goal for the trip overall, but not a requirement at every single stop.

objectives.name: Food index
objectives.desc: Eat like locals -- markets, tavernas, street food. No tourist traps.

objectives.name: Cost efficiency index
objectives.desc: Max experience per dollar. Rural Greece beats Rome badly here.

objectives.name: Travel simplicity index
objectives.desc: Minimize painful transit -- long waits and rushed city-to-city moves drain the trip.

objectives.name: Group harmony index
objectives.desc: Teens engaged + adults not bored. Mix of challenge and depth. Teens specifically ranked exploring a big city on their own as MORE exciting than swimming, glaciers, or hiking -- build in unstructured/independent time in Athens and Naples.

objectives.name: Variety index
objectives.desc: Not every stop should feel like the same Mediterranean postcard. Snow, mountains, and a modern city (like Switzerland offers) count as real trip value, distinct from coast/history/food.

open_questions: Naples is now the baseline Italy stop (Rome lost group support) -- any objections?
Worth swapping in Switzerland (mountains/glaciers) despite it being ~2x the cost of every other option?
Comfortable with narrow mountain driving in the Peloponnese?
Everyone in for the Samaria Gorge hike (16km, ~6hrs)?
Ryanair (cheaper, bag fees) vs full-service airlines for intra-Europe legs?
Exact dates -- mid-June (cooler, cheaper) vs July (hotter, pricier)?
crew_text:
OUR TRIP OBJECTIVES -- REACT / ADD BELOW

GROUP: 3 adults + 3 teens (13-15), able-bodied, good travel problem-solvers
WINDOW: Mid-June to late July
BUDGET: $10-15K per family of 4 (2 adults + 2 teens)

1. EXPLORATION INDEX
Places none of us have been. Greece = high (first time) for everyone. Rome, French Riviera, and Italian Riviera are all lower here since 2 of the 6 travelers have already been to all three.

2. HISTORY & CULTURE INDEX
Real ruins, castles, archaeological sites -- walk through the story, not just photos. Note: teens ranked ancient ruins LOWEST of 5 priorities when asked what they'd miss most if cut -- adults may value this more than teens do.

3. OUTDOOR / ACTIVE INDEX
Hiking, swimming, physical challenge. Teens want to move.

4. MEDITERRANEAN COAST INDEX
Turquoise water, beaches, cliffs -- a significant goal for the trip overall, but not a requirement at every single stop.

5. FOOD INDEX
Eat like locals -- markets, tavernas, street food. No tourist traps.

6. COST EFFICIENCY INDEX
Max experience per dollar. Rural Greece beats Rome badly here.

7. TRAVEL SIMPLICITY INDEX
Minimize painful transit -- long waits and rushed city-to-city moves drain the trip.

8. GROUP HARMONY INDEX
Teens engaged + adults not bored. Mix of challenge and depth. Teens specifically ranked exploring a big city on their own as MORE exciting than swimming, glaciers, or hiking -- build in unstructured/independent time in Athens and Naples.

9. VARIETY INDEX
Not every stop should feel like the same Mediterranean postcard. Snow, mountains, and a modern city (like Switzerland offers) count as real trip value, distinct from coast/history/food.

OPEN QUESTIONS FOR THE GROUP:
- Naples is now the baseline Italy stop (Rome lost group support) -- any objections?
- Worth swapping in Switzerland (mountains/glaciers) despite it being ~2x the cost of every other option?
- Comfortable with narrow mountain driving in the Peloponnese?
- Everyone in for the Samaria Gorge hike (16km, ~6hrs)?
- Ryanair (cheaper, bag fees) vs full-service airlines for intra-Europe legs?
- Exact dates -- mid-June (cooler, cheaper) vs July (hotter, pricier)?

Reply with your top 3 priorities from the list above, or add anything missing.

cut_priority_ranking: Beach/swim day (would be missed MOST if cut)
Ancient ruins -- Pompeii/Acropolis/Mycenae (would be missed LEAST if cut)
teen_feedback.switzerland_verdict: Conditional yes -- only if kept to 2-3 nights max, not extended
teen_feedback.most_exciting_anticipated: Clarified after follow-up: 'exploring a big city' specifically meant flea markets / junk shops / thrift shopping, not generic city wandering.

what_theyre_looking_for: Random one-of-a-kind junk, don't care what
market_preferences.buy_or_browse: Buy stuff -- worth the luggage space
market_preferences.naples_vs_athens: Both equally -- try to hit whichever lines up with actual travel dates

implications: Ancient ruins ranked LAST despite adults prioritizing history heavily (objective 2) -- worth checking whether ruin-heavy days need trimming or reframing for teen engagement.

The #2 priority is specifically flea markets and junk shops, not generic city time -- Naples now has a real flea market attraction added (Mercatino dell'Antiquariato), and Athens' Monastiraki is exactly this kind of stop.
They want to BUY, not just browse -- pack an empty duffel or expect to buy a cheap bag abroad; factor this into Ryanair/budget-airline baggage fee math on the intra-Europe legs.
Half a day is a real time block -- this should show up as scheduled time in Naples and/or Athens, not squeezed into leftover hours.

Naples' market is only open specific weekends of the month -- MUST check actual travel dates against this before counting on it. If dates don't line up, Athens' daily Monastiraki becomes the reliable fallback.
Switzerland's 3-night length is already at the edge of teen tolerance -- do not extend it if it's added.
